comparisons.what_meant: I want to tease a lesson about pricing mistakes.
comparisons.what_heard: Clickbait headline with no real promise.
comparisons.rewrite: Most founders lose margin before the sale even starts. Tomorrow I'll show the one sentence that causes it.
comparisons.annotation: Good micro-cliffhanger: clear topic, concrete stake, and a promised reveal in the next step.

comparisons.what_meant: I want to open a story about a failed launch.
comparisons.rewrite: We hit 10,000 signups in 48 hours—and still called the launch a failure. Here's the hidden metric that forced that decision.
comparisons.annotation: Good curiosity gap: tension is specific, and the audience knows exactly why the answer matters.

comparisons.what_meant: I want readers to stay for a full case study.
comparisons.what_heard: Long post warning, likely skimmable and generic.
comparisons.rewrite: A client said one sentence in week 1 that predicted the entire project outcome. Keep it in mind while you read this case.
comparisons.annotation: Narrative cliffhanger: plants a question early so readers keep scanning for resolution.

before_after.before: You won't believe what happened next.
before_after.after: At 4:52 PM the deal looked done. At 4:53 PM one reply changed the contract terms.
before_after.annotation: Bad is empty hype. Good adds a concrete scene so curiosity feels earned.

before_after.after: If your opening line does not create a question, your reader has no reason to keep going. Try this 3-part hook.
before_after.annotation: Good framing states the rule, the consequence, and a usable next step.

before_after.before: This founder made a big mistake.
before_after.after: She ignored one customer email for 11 days. That delay cost a six-figure renewal.
before_after.annotation: Specific stakes beat vague drama; readers understand why they should care.

playground.base_text: I help experienced professionals communicate their expertise online.
